title: How is VAT calculated on my electricity bill?
text: Value Added Tax (VAT) is applied to your electricity bill at the standard rate of 13% for residential customers and 24% for business customers in Greece. VAT is calculated on the combined subtotal of your energy charges and regulated network charges (UoS charges). It does not apply separately to each line item but rather to the taxable base, which is the sum of energy consumption charges and network use charges. VAT appears as a single line item on your bill labelled 'VAT' or 'ΦΠΑ'. For example: if your energy charge is 50.40 EUR and regulated charges are 30.00 EUR, the taxable base is 80.40 EUR and VAT at 13% is 10.45 EUR. The standing charge (fixed monthly fee) is also subject to VAT. The renewable energy levy (ETMEAR) and other levies may or may not be subject to VAT depending on current regulation — check the itemised bill for clarity. If you are a business customer registered for VAT, you may be able to reclaim the VAT charged on your electricity bill through your standard VAT return.
